title: Amchang Wildlife Sanctuary
tagline: Amchang Wildlife Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Assam

overview.short: The Amchang Wildlife Sanctuary is a wildlife sanctuary located on the eastern fringe of Guwahati, Assam, India.
overview.description: The Amchang Wildlife Sanctuary is a wildlife sanctuary located on the eastern fringe of Guwahati, Assam, India. The name Amchang is originated from the name of Arleng village named Amcheng (now Amsing) located in the heart of the wildlife sanctuary. The sanctuary is known for hosting rare and endangered birds and animals. Amchang's habitat is dominated by tropical moist deciduous forest with semi-evergreen forest in depressions and river-valleys.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Tinali
topPlaces.description: Narangi Tinali is a locality of Guwahati, India, in the state of Assam. It is near the locality of Narengi.

topPlaces.name: Udayan Vihar
topPlaces.description: Udayan Vihar is a locality in Guwahati, surrounded by localities of Satgaon, Narengi and Noonmati.

topPlaces.name: Gandhinagar, Guwahati
topPlaces.description: Gandhinagar is a locality in Guwahati. Located in east of the city, it is sparsely populated.

topPlaces.name: Panikhaiti
topPlaces.description: Panikhaiti is a village in Kamrup, situated on the south bank of the Brahmaputra River.

topPlaces.name: Silsako Lake
topPlaces.description: Silsako Lake (also known as Silsako Beel) is an urban wetland and lake located at the heart of the Guwahati city positioned between 26°10'08.47" N and 91°49'45.53" E. It is an elongated, linear-shaped beel surrounded by Amchang Wildlife Sanctuary to the east, with VIP Road and Chachal to the west, Narengi to the north, and Panjabari to the south. It is surrounded by villages like Satgaon, Hengrabari and Mathgharia in Kamrup Metropolitan district of Assam.

topPlaces.name: Noonmati
topPlaces.description: Noonmati is a locality located in Guwahati, Assam in North East India. It is the place where Indian Oil Company (now the Indian Oil Corporation) commissioned its first oil refinery, the Guwahati Refinery on 1 January 1962.

topPlaces.name: Hengrabari
topPlaces.description: Hengrabari is a district in the capital city of Guwahati of Assam, a state on the north-east of India. Densely populated, Hengrabari resembles the character of a small town in the heart of Guwahati.

topPlaces.name: Srimanta Sankardev Kalakshetra
topPlaces.description: Srimanta Sankaradev Kalakshetra, commonly known as Kalakshetra, is a cultural institution in the Panjabari area of Guwahati, Assam, India, named after the medieval poet-playwright and reformer Srimanta Sankardev. It includes a cultural museum, library, a children's park, and various facilities for preserving, demonstrating and performing cultural items and history.

It is an elongated, linear-shaped beel surrounded by Amchang Wildlife Sanctuary to the east, with VIP Road and Chachal to the west, Narengi to the north, and Panjabari to the south. faq.q: What is the best time to visit Amchang Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Amchang Wildlife Sanctuary is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Amchang Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Lokpriya Gopinath Bordoloi International Airport, Guwahati (29 km) and the nearest railway station is Guwahati Railway Station (11 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Amchang Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Amchang Wildlife Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Amchang Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Amchang Wildlife Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Amchang Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Tinali, Udayan Vihar, Gandhinagar, Guwahati, Panikhaiti, Silsako Lake.
